title: NOC 22113 – Conservation and Fishery Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Conservation and Fishery Officers are in rising demand across Canada, supported by wildlife-protection mandates, fisheries enforcement programs, environmental-monitoring systems, and climate-resilience strategies. Classified under NOC 22113 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Promote conservation awareness and legal compliance
Patrol protected areas by vehicle, boat, aircraft, or foot
Investigate violations and present evidence in court
Issue licenses, export permits, and royalty documents
Monitor fishing activity and inspect gear
Conduct fish inventories and water sampling
employment_requirements: College diploma in renewable-resources management, environmental conservation, or a related field
Some roles require pesticide-applicator or explosives licences
job_outlook: This supply gap makes the occupation particularly attractive for candidates with:
pathways: Long-term roles in conservation and sustainability
additional_information: Provincial conservation servicesFisheries-enforcement unitsWildlife-management agenciesNational & provincial parksEnvironmental-protection departmentsNorthern & coastal monitoring programs
Annual salaries commonly range from $40,500–$86,600 CAD, depending on province, posting location, and enforcement specialization.
